top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 110, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
16,990ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
154ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 110 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
16,990ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes. Train it until it feels boring.
La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes' paved Alpine mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 110 miles.
110 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Low-cadence force work for 16,990ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Alpine mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: June in Bourg d'Oisans, Isere, France — A single day spanning near-freezing summit conditions at 2,642m and potential 35C (95F) heat on Alpe d'Huez. The Galibier summit can see snow, ice, and violent winds even in late June. Valley starts are cool (15-20C) but warm rapidly. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day. Race-day realities: Alpe d'Huez in afternoon heat: potentially 30-35C (86-95F).
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes’s 110 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes with 16,990ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Galibier summit: 5-10C with wind chill well below zero; Sudden thunderstorms build from noon onward — lightning at altitude is real.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 110 miles of paved alpine mountain passes in Bourg d'Oisans, Isere, France.
